seoTitle: How to Change Count to Sum in Power BI Quickly
metaDescription: Fix incorrect aggregation in Power BI by switching a field from Count to Sum, so totals in your visuals reflect actual values instead of row counts.
intro: It's a common early mistake: a numeric field gets added to a visual and Power BI defaults to counting rows instead of summing the actual values. This guide shows how to quickly change that aggregation from Count to Sum. It's a small fix, but one that matters a lot for anyone relying on accurate totals in sales, inventory, or financial reports.

steps.heading: Navigate the Power BI interface
steps.body: Click on the visual showing the incorrect aggregation and locate the field in the Values well of the visualizations pane.

steps.heading: Change Count to Sum
steps.body: Click the dropdown arrow next to the field in the Values well and select Sum from the list of available aggregation types.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for data visualization
steps.body: Double-check that the field is truly numeric, since text or ID fields will only offer count-based aggregations rather than sum.

steps.heading: Verify the corrected totals
steps.body: Compare the new summed value against a known total from your source data to confirm the aggregation change produced accurate results.

faq.question: Why did Power BI default to Count instead of Sum in the first place?
faq.answer: Power BI often defaults numeric fields imported in certain formats, or fields it interprets as identifiers, to Count rather than Sum until you manually change it.

faq.question: Can I set the default aggregation for a field so I don't have to fix it every time?
faq.answer: Yes, in the Data view you can select the column and change its default summarization setting so new visuals use Sum automatically going forward.

faq.question: Does changing Count to Sum affect other visuals using the same field?
faq.answer: No, changing the aggregation within a single visual only affects that visual, unless you change the column's default summarization at the model level.

faq.question: Why is the Sum option grayed out for my field?
faq.answer: This usually means the field is stored as text rather than a numeric data type, which needs to be corrected in Power Query before Sum becomes available.
